
UnmarshalText unmarshals a text representation to the enum value. It returns an error if given a wrong value.

<a name="Locator"></a>
## type [Locator](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L19-L27>)

Locator represents a way to find element\(s\) on the page at any moment.

```go
type Locator struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewLocator"></a>
### func [NewLocator](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L40>)

```go
func NewLocator(ctx context.Context, opts *LocatorOptions, selector string, f *Frame, l *log.Logger) *Locator
```

NewLocator creates and returns a new locator.

<a name="Locator.All"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [All](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L103>)

```go
func (l *Locator) All() ([]*Locator, error)
```



<a name="Locator.BoundingBox"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [BoundingBox](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L60>)

```go
func (l *Locator) BoundingBox(opts *FrameBaseOptions) (*Rect, error)
```

BoundingBox will return the bounding box of the element.

<a name="Locator.Check"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Check](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L176>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Check(opts *FrameCheckOptions) error
```

Check on an element using locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.Clear"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Clear](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L67>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Clear(opts *FrameFillOptions) error
```

Clear will clear the input field. This works with the Fill API and fills the input field with an empty string.

<a name="Locator.Click"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Click](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L87>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Click(opts *FrameClickOptions) error
```

Click on an element using locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.ContentFrame"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [ContentFrame](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L122>)

```go
func (l *Locator) ContentFrame() *FrameLocator
```

ContentFrame creates and returns a new FrameLocator, which is useful when needing to interact with elements in an iframe and the current locator already points to the iframe.

<a name="Locator.Count"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Count](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L129>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Count() (int, error)
```

Count APIs do not wait for the element to be present. It also does not set strict to true, allowing it to return the total number of elements matching the selector.

<a name="Locator.Dblclick"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Dblclick](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L134>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Dblclick(opts *FrameDblclickOptions) error
```

Dblclick double clicks on an element using locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.DefaultTimeout"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [DefaultTimeout](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L646>)

```go
func (l *Locator) DefaultTimeout() time.Duration
```

DefaultTimeout returns the default timeout for the locator. This is an internal API and should not be used by users.

<a name="Locator.DispatchEvent"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [DispatchEvent](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L615>)

```go
func (l *Locator) DispatchEvent(typ string, eventInit any, opts *FrameDispatchEventOptions) error
```

DispatchEvent dispatches an event for the element matching the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.Evaluate"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Evaluate](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L148>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Evaluate(pageFunc string, args ...any) (any, error)
```



<a name="Locator.EvaluateHandle"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [EvaluateHandle](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L152>)

```go
func (l *Locator) EvaluateHandle(pageFunc string, args ...any) (JSHandleAPI, error)
```



<a name="Locator.Fill"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Fill](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L288>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Fill(value string, opts *FrameFillOptions) error
```

Fill out the element using locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.Filter"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Filter](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L311>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Filter(opts *LocatorFilterOptions) *Locator
```

Filter returns a new [Locator](<#Locator>) after applying the options to the current one.

<a name="Locator.First"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [First](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L317>)

```go
func (l *Locator) First() *Locator
```

First will return the first child of the element matching the locator's selector.

<a name="Locator.Focus"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Focus](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L322>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Focus(opts *FrameBaseOptions) error
```

Focus on the element using locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.FrameLocator"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [FrameLocator](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L430>)

```go
func (l *Locator) FrameLocator(selector string) *FrameLocator
```

FrameLocator creates a frame locator for an iframe matching the given selector within the current locator's scope.

<a name="Locator.GetAttribute"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetAttribute](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L337>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetAttribute(name string, opts *FrameBaseOptions) (string, bool, error)
```

GetAttribute of the element using locator's selector with strict mode on. The second return value is true if the attribute exists, and false otherwise.

<a name="Locator.GetByAltText"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByAltText](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L353>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByAltText(alt string, opts *GetByBaseOptions) *Locator
```

GetByAltText creates and returns a new relative locator that allows locating elements by their alt text.

<a name="Locator.GetByLabel"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByLabel](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L364>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByLabel(label string, opts *GetByBaseOptions) *Locator
```

GetByLabel creates and returns a new relative locator that allows locating input elements by the text of the associated \`\<label\>\` or \`aria\-labelledby\` element, or by the \`aria\-label\` attribute.

<a name="Locator.GetByPlaceholder"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByPlaceholder](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L374>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByPlaceholder(placeholder string, opts *GetByBaseOptions) *Locator
```

GetByPlaceholder creates and returns a new relative locator for this based on the placeholder attribute.

<a name="Locator.GetByRole"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByRole](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L384>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByRole(role string, opts *GetByRoleOptions) *Locator
```

GetByRole creates and returns a new relative locator using the ARIA role and any additional options.

<a name="Locator.GetByTestID"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByTestID](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L394>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByTestID(testID string) *Locator
```

GetByTestID creates and returns a new relative locator based on the data\-testid attribute.

<a name="Locator.GetByText"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByText](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L404>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByText(text string, opts *GetByBaseOptions) *Locator
```

GetByText creates and returns a new relative locator based on text content.

<a name="Locator.GetByTitle"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [GetByTitle](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L414>)

```go
func (l *Locator) GetByTitle(title string, opts *GetByBaseOptions) *Locator
```

GetByTitle creates and returns a new relative locator based on the title attribute.

<a name="Locator.Hover"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Hover](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L584>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Hover(opts *FrameHoverOptions) error
```

Hover moves the pointer over the element that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.InnerHTML"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [InnerHTML](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L438>)

```go
func (l *Locator) InnerHTML(opts *FrameInnerHTMLOptions) (string, error)
```

InnerHTML returns the element's inner HTML that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.InnerText"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [InnerText](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L452>)

```go
func (l *Locator) InnerText(opts *FrameInnerTextOptions) (string, error)
```

InnerText returns the element's inner text that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.InputValue"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [InputValue](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L494>)

```go
func (l *Locator) InputValue(opts *FrameInputValueOptions) (string, error)
```

InputValue returns the element's input value that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.IsChecked"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [IsChecked](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L207>)

```go
func (l *Locator) IsChecked(opts *FrameIsCheckedOptions) (bool, error)
```

IsChecked returns true if the element matches the locator's selector and is checked. Otherwise, returns false.

<a name="Locator.IsDisabled"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [IsDisabled](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L249>)

```go
func (l *Locator) IsDisabled(opts *FrameIsDisabledOptions) (bool, error)
```

IsDisabled returns true if the element matches the locator's selector and is disabled. Otherwise, returns false.

<a name="Locator.IsEditable"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [IsEditable](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L221>)

```go
func (l *Locator) IsEditable(opts *FrameIsEditableOptions) (bool, error)
```

IsEditable returns true if the element matches the locator's selector and is Editable. Otherwise, returns false.

<a name="Locator.IsEnabled"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [IsEnabled](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L235>)

```go
func (l *Locator) IsEnabled(opts *FrameIsEnabledOptions) (bool, error)
```

IsEnabled returns true if the element matches the locator's selector and is Enabled. Otherwise, returns false.

<a name="Locator.IsHidden"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [IsHidden](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L276>)

```go
func (l *Locator) IsHidden() (bool, error)
```

IsHidden returns true if the element matches the locator's selector and is hidden. Otherwise, returns false.

<a name="Locator.IsVisible"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [IsVisible](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L263>)

```go
func (l *Locator) IsVisible() (bool, error)
```

IsVisible returns true if the element matches the locator's selector and is visible. Otherwise, returns false.

<a name="Locator.Last"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Last](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L466>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Last() *Locator
```

Last will return the last child of the element matching the locator's selector.

<a name="Locator.Locator"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Locator](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L424>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Locator(selector string, opts *LocatorOptions) *Locator
```

Locator creates and returns a new locator chained/relative to the current locator.

<a name="Locator.Nth"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Nth](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L472>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Nth(nth int) *Locator
```

Nth will return the nth child of the element matching the locator's selector.

<a name="Locator.Press"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Press](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L525>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Press(key string, opts *FramePressOptions) error
```

Press the given key on the element found that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.PressSequentially"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [PressSequentially](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L544>)

```go
func (l *Locator) PressSequentially(text string, opts *FrameTypeOptions) error
```

PressSequentially focuses on the element and sequentially sends a keydown, keypress, and keyup events for each character in the provided string. For handling special keys, use the [Locator.Press](<#Locator.Press>) method.

<a name="Locator.SelectOption"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [SelectOption](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L509>)

```go
func (l *Locator) SelectOption(values []any, opts *FrameSelectOptionOptions) ([]string, error)
```

SelectOption filters option values of the first element that matches the locator's selector \(with strict mode on\), selects the options, and returns the filtered options.

<a name="Locator.SetChecked"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [SetChecked](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L158>)

```go
func (l *Locator) SetChecked(checked bool, opts *FrameCheckOptions) error
```

SetChecked sets the checked state of the element using locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.Tap"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [Tap](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L599>)

```go
func (l *Locator) Tap(opts *FrameTapOptions) error
```

Tap the element found that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

<a name="Locator.TextContent"></a>
### func \(\*Locator\) [TextContent](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L480>)

```go
func (l *Locator) TextContent(opts *FrameTextContentOptions) (string, bool, error)
```

TextContent returns the element's text content that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on. The second return value is true if the returned text content is not null or empty, and false otherwise.

<a name="Locator.Timeout"></a>

```go
func (l *Locator) Timeout() time.Duration
```

Timeout will return the default timeout or the one set by the user.

```go
func (l *Locator) Type(text string, opts *FrameTypeOptions) error
```

Type text on the element found that matches the locator's selector with strict mode on.

```go
func (l *Locator) Uncheck(opts *FrameUncheckOptions) error
```

<a name="LocatorFilterOptions"></a>
## type [LocatorFilterOptions](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L306-L308>)

LocatorFilterOptions allows filtering a [Locator](<#Locator>) by various criteria. It's similar to [LocatorOptions](<#LocatorOptions>) but used for filtering existing locators.

```go
type LocatorFilterOptions struct {
    *LocatorOptions
}
```

<a name="LocatorOptions"></a>
## type [LocatorOptions](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/locator.go#L30-L37>)

LocatorOptions allows modifying the [Locator](<#Locator>) behavior.

```go
type LocatorOptions struct {
    // Matches only elements that contain the specified text.
    // String or RegExp. Optional.
    HasText string
    // Matches only elements that do not contain the specified text.
    // String or RegExp. Optional.
    HasNotText string
}
```

<a name="Match"></a>
## type [Match](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/page.go#L431-L436>)

Match contains the fields that will be used to match against metric tags that are about to be emitted.

```go
type Match struct {
    // This is a regex that will be compared against the existing url tag.
    URLRegEx string `js:"url"`
    // This is the request method to match on.
    Method string `js:"method"`
}
```

<a name="MediaType"></a>
## type [MediaType](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/page.go#L70>)

MediaType represents the type of media to emulate.

```go
type MediaType string
```

<a name="MediaTypeScreen"></a>

```go
const (
    // MediaTypeScreen represents the screen media type.
    MediaTypeScreen MediaType = "screen"

    // MediaTypePrint represents the print media type.
    MediaTypePrint MediaType = "print"
)
```

```go
type MetricEvent struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```


```go
type NetworkProfile struct {

    Upload float64
}
```

<a name="PageEvent"></a>
## type [PageEvent](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/page.go#L1405-L1418>)

PageEvent represents a generic page event. Use one of the fields to get the specific event data.

```go
type PageEvent struct {
    // ConsoleMessage is the console message event.
    ConsoleMessage *ConsoleMessage

    // Metric is the metric event event.
    Metric *MetricEvent

    // Request is the read only request that is about to be sent from the
    // browser to the WuT.
    Request *Request

    // Response is the read only response that was received from the WuT.
    Response *Response
}
```

<a name="PageEventHandler"></a>
## type [PageEventHandler](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/page.go#L60>)

PageEventHandler is a function type that handles a page on event.

```go
type PageEventHandler func(PageEvent) error
```

<a name="PageEventName"></a>
## type [PageEventName](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/page.go#L35>)

PageEventName represents the name of the page event.

```go
type PageEventName string
```

<a name="PageEventConsole"></a>

```go
const (
    // PageEventConsole represents the page console event.
    PageEventConsole PageEventName = "console"

    // PageEventMetric represents the page metric event.
    PageEventMetric PageEventName = "metric"

    // PageEventRequest represents the page request event.
    PageEventRequest PageEventName = "request"

    // PageEventResponse represents the page response event.
    PageEventResponse PageEventName = "response"

    // PageEventRequestFinished represents the page request finished event.
    PageEventRequestFinished PageEventName = "requestfinished"

    // PageEventRequestFailed represents the page requestfailed event.
    PageEventRequestFailed PageEventName = "requestfailed"
)
```

<a name="Request.PostData"></a>
### func \(\*Request\) [PostData](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/http.go#L316>)

```go
func (r *Request) PostData() string
```

PostData returns the request post data, if any.

If will not attempt to fetch the data if it should have some but nothing is cached locally: https://go.k6.io/k6/js/modules/k6/browser/issues/1470

This relies on PostDataEntries. It will only ever return the 0th entry. TODO: Create a PostDataEntries API when we have a better idea of when that is needed.

<a name="Request.PostDataBuffer"></a>
### func \(\*Request\) [PostDataBuffer](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/http.go#L332>)

```go
func (r *Request) PostDataBuffer() []byte
```

PostDataBuffer returns the request post data as an ArrayBuffer.

If will not attempt to fetch the data if it should have some but nothing is cached locally: https://go.k6.io/k6/js/modules/k6/browser/issues/1470

This relies on PostDataEntries. It will only ever return the 0th entry. TODO: Create a PostDataEntries API when we have a better idea of when that is needed.

<a name="ScrollIntoViewOptions"></a>
## type [ScrollIntoViewOptions](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/element_handle_options.go#L55-L65>)

ScrollIntoViewOptions change the behavior of ScrollIntoView. See: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/scrollIntoView

```go
type ScrollIntoViewOptions struct {
    // Block defines vertical alignment.
    // One of start, center, end, or nearest.
    // Defaults to start.
    Block ScrollPosition `json:"block"`

    // Inline defines horizontal alignment.
    // One of start, center, end, or nearest.
    // Defaults to nearest.
    Inline ScrollPosition `json:"inline"`
}
```


<a name="ScrollPositionStart"></a>

```go
const (
    // ScrollPositionStart scrolls an element at the top of its parent.
    ScrollPositionStart ScrollPosition = "start"
    // ScrollPositionCenter scrolls an element at the center of its parent.
    ScrollPositionCenter ScrollPosition = "center"
    // ScrollPositionEnd scrolls an element at the end of its parent.
    ScrollPositionEnd ScrollPosition = "end"
    // ScrollPositionNearest scrolls an element at the nearest position of its parent.
    ScrollPositionNearest ScrollPosition = "nearest"
)
```

# js

```go
import "go.k6.io/k6/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/common/js"
```

Package js provides JavaScript code that the browser module evaluates on the browser. The common package uses this package. \`injected\_script.js\` is injected into each execution context.

```go
var WebVitalIIFEScript string
```


# env

```go
import "go.k6.io/k6/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/env"
```

Package env provides types to interact with environment setup.

## Index

- [Constants](<#constants>)
- [func EmptyLookup\(\_ string\) \(string, bool\)](<#EmptyLookup>)
- [func IsBrowserHeadless\(\) bool](<#IsBrowserHeadless>)
- [func Lookup\(key string\) \(string, bool\)](<#Lookup>)
- [func LookupBool\(key string\) \(value bool, ok bool\)](<#LookupBool>)
- [type LookupFunc](<#LookupFunc>)
  - [func ConstLookup\(k, v string\) LookupFunc](<#ConstLookup>)


## Constants

<a name="InstanceScenarios"></a>Execution specific.

```go
const (
    // InstanceScenarios is an environment variable that can be used to
    // define the extra scenarios details to use when running remotely.
    InstanceScenarios = "K6_INSTANCE_SCENARIOS"

    // WebSocketURLs is an environment variable that can be used to
    // define the WS URLs to connect to when running remotely.
    WebSocketURLs = "K6_BROWSER_WS_URL"

    // BrowserArguments is an environment variable that can be used to
    // pass extra arguments to the browser process.
    BrowserArguments = "K6_BROWSER_ARGS"

    // BrowserExecutablePath is an environment variable that can be used
    // to define the path to the browser to execute.
    BrowserExecutablePath = "K6_BROWSER_EXECUTABLE_PATH"

    // BrowserEnableDebugging is an environment variable that can be used to
    // define if the browser should be launched with debugging enabled.
    BrowserEnableDebugging = "K6_BROWSER_DEBUG"

    // BrowserHeadless is an environment variable that can be used to
    // define if the browser should be launched in headless mode.
    BrowserHeadless = "K6_BROWSER_HEADLESS"

    // BrowserIgnoreDefaultArgs is an environment variable that can be
    // used to define if the browser should ignore default arguments.
    BrowserIgnoreDefaultArgs = "K6_BROWSER_IGNORE_DEFAULT_ARGS"

    // BrowserGlobalTimeout is an environment variable that can be used
    // to set the global timeout for the browser.
    BrowserGlobalTimeout = "K6_BROWSER_TIMEOUT"
)
```

<a name="LogCaller"></a>Logging and debugging.

```go
const (
    // LogCaller is an environment variable that can be used to enable
    // the caller function information in the browser logs.
    LogCaller = "K6_BROWSER_LOG_CALLER"

    // LogLevel is an environment variable that can be used to set the
    // log level for the browser logs.
    LogLevel = "K6_BROWSER_LOG"

    // LogCategoryFilter is an environment variable that can be used to
    // filter the browser logs based on their category. It supports
    // regular expressions.
    LogCategoryFilter = "K6_BROWSER_LOG_CATEGORY_FILTER"
)
```

<a name="K6TestRunID"></a>Infrastructural.

```go
const (
    // K6TestRunID represents the test run id. Note: this was taken from
    // k6.
    K6TestRunID = "K6_CLOUD_PUSH_REF_ID"
)
```

<a name="ScreenshotsOutput"></a>Screenshots.

```go
const (
    // ScreenshotsOutput can be used to configure the browser module
    // to upload screenshots to a remote location instead of saving
    // to the local disk.
    ScreenshotsOutput = "K6_BROWSER_SCREENSHOTS_OUTPUT"
)
```

<a name="TracesMetadata"></a>Tracing.

```go
const (
    // TracesMetadata is an environment variable that can be used to
    // set additional metadata to be included in the generated traces.
    // The format must comply with: key1=value1,key2=value2,...
    TracesMetadata = "K6_BROWSER_TRACES_METADATA"
)
```

<a name="EmptyLookup"></a>
## func [EmptyLookup](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/env/env.go#L87>)

```go
func EmptyLookup(_ string) (string, bool)
```

EmptyLookup is a LookupFunc that always returns "" and false.

IsBrowserHeadless returns true if the BrowserHeadless environment variable is not set or set to true. The default behaviour is to run the browser in headless mode.

<a name="LookupBool"></a>
## func [LookupBool](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/env/env.go#L107>)

```go
func LookupBool(key string) (value bool, ok bool)
```

LookupBool returns the result of Lookup as a bool. If the key does not exist or the value is not a valid bool, it returns false. Otherwise it returns the bool value and true.

<a name="LookupFunc"></a>
## type [LookupFunc](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/env/env.go#L84>)

LookupFunc defines a function to look up a key from the environment.

```go
type LookupFunc func(key string) (string, bool)
```

<a name="ConstLookup"></a>
### func [ConstLookup](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/env/env.go#L95>)

```go
func ConstLookup(k, v string) LookupFunc
```

ConstLookup is a LookupFunc that always returns the given value and true if the key matches the given key. Otherwise it returns EmptyLookup behaviour. Useful for testing.

# k6error

```go
import "go.k6.io/k6/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6error"
```

Package k6error contains ErrFatal.

## Index

- [Variables](<#variables>)


## Variables

<a name="ErrFatal"></a>ErrFatal should be wrapped into an error to signal to the mapping layer that the error is a fatal error and we should abort the whole test run, not just the current iteration. It should be used in cases where if the iteration ran again then there's a 100% chance that it will end up running into the same error.

```go
var ErrFatal = errors.New("fatal error")
```

<a name="Abortf"></a>


## type [VU](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6ext/k6test/vu.go#L31-L37>)

VU is a k6 VU instance. TODO: Do we still need this VU wrapper? ToSobekValue can be a helper function that takes a sobek.Runtime \(although it's not much of a helper from calling ToValue\(i\) directly...\), and we can access EventLoop from modulestest.Runtime.EventLoop.

```go
type VU struct {
    *k6modulestest.VU
    Loop *eventloop.EventLoop

    TestRT *k6modulestest.Runtime
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewVU"></a>
### func [NewVU](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6ext/k6test/vu.go#L186>)

```go
func NewVU(tb testing.TB, opts ...any) *VU
```

NewVU returns a mock k6 VU.

opts can be one of the following:

- WithSamples: a bidirectional channel that will be used to emit metrics.
- env.LookupFunc: a lookup function that will be used to lookup environment variables.
- WithTracerProvider: a TracerProvider that will be set as the VU TracerProvider.

<a name="VU.ActivateVU"></a>
### func \(\*VU\) [ActivateVU](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6ext/k6test/vu.go#L45>)

```go
func (v *VU) ActivateVU()
```

ActivateVU mimicks activation of the VU as in k6. It transitions the VU from the init stage to the execution stage by setting the VU's state to the state that was passed to NewVU.

<a name="VU.AssertSamples"></a>
### func \(\*VU\) [AssertSamples](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6ext/k6test/vu.go#L52>)

```go
func (v *VU) AssertSamples(assertSample func(s k6metrics.Sample)) int
```

AssertSamples asserts each sample VU received since AssertSamples is last called, then it returns the number of received samples.

<a name="VU.EndIteration"></a>
### func \(\*VU\) [EndIteration](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6ext/k6test/vu.go#L93>)

```go
func (v *VU) EndIteration(tb testing.TB, opts ...any)
```

EndIteration generates a new IterEnd event through the VU event system.

opts can be used to parameterize the iteration data such as:

- WithScenarioName: sets the scenario name \(default is 'default'\).
- WithVUID: sets the VUID \(default 1\).
- WithIteration: sets the iteration \(default 0\).

<a name="VU.RunAsync"></a>
### func \(\*VU\) [RunAsync](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/k6ext/k6test/vu.go#L138>)

```go
func (v *VU) RunAsync(tb testing.TB, js string, args ...any) (sobek.Value, error)
```

RunAsync runs the given JavaScript code on the VU's event loop and returns the result as a sobek.Value.

```go
func (v *VU) RunOnEventLoop(tb testing.TB, js string, args ...any) (sobek.Value, error)
```

RunOnEventLoop runs the given JavaScript code on the VU's event loop and returns the result as a sobek.Value.


RunPromise runs the given JavaScript code on the VU's event loop and returns the result as a \*sobek.Promise.

<a name="K6BrowserDataDirPattern"></a>K6BrowserDataDirPattern is the pattern used to create the temporary directory for the browser data.

```go
const K6BrowserDataDirPattern = "k6browser-data-*"
```

<a name="Dir"></a>
## type [Dir](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/storage/storage.go#L21-L29>)

Dir manages data storage for the extension and user specific data on the local filesystem.

```go
type Dir struct {
    Dir string // path to the data storage directory
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Dir.Cleanup"></a>
### func \(\*Dir\) [Cleanup](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/storage/storage.go#L70>)

```go
func (d *Dir) Cleanup() error
```

Cleanup removes the temporary directory if Make was called with a non empty dir argument. It is named as Cleanup because it can be used for other features in the future.

<a name="Dir.Make"></a>
### func \(\*Dir\) [Make](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/storage/storage.go#L34>)

```go
func (d *Dir) Make(tmpDir string, dir any) error
```

Make creates a new temporary directory in tmpDir, and stores the path to the directory in the Dir field. When the dir argument is not empty, no directory will be created and it will not be deleted if Cleanup is called.

<a name="Tracer"></a>
## type [Tracer](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/trace/trace.go#L34-L41>)

Tracer represents a traces generator tailored to k6 browser needs. Specifically implements methods to generate spans for navigations, API calls and page events, accepting input parameters that allow correlating async operations, such as Web Vitals events, with the page to which they belong to.

```go
type Tracer struct {
    trace.Tracer
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTracer"></a>
### func [NewTracer](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/trace/trace.go#L44>)

```go
func NewTracer(tp k6lib.TracerProvider, metadata map[string]string, options ...trace.TracerOption) *Tracer
```

NewTracer creates a new Tracer from the given TracerProvider.

<a name="Tracer.Start"></a>
### func \(\*Tracer\) [Start](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/trace/trace.go#L53-L55>)

```go
func (t *Tracer) Start(ctx context.Context, spanName string, opts ...trace.SpanStartOption) (context.Context, trace.Span)
```

Start overrides the underlying OTEL tracer method to include the tracer metadata.

<a name="Tracer.TraceAPICall"></a>
### func \(\*Tracer\) [TraceAPICall](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/trace/trace.go#L65-L67>)

```go
func (t *Tracer) TraceAPICall(ctx context.Context, targetID string, spanName string, opts ...trace.SpanStartOption) (context.Context, trace.Span)
```

TraceAPICall adds a new span to the current liveSpan for the given targetID and returns it. It is the caller's responsibility to close the generated span. If there is not a liveSpan for the given targetID, the new span is created based on the given context, which means that it might be a root span or not depending if the context already wraps a span.

<a name="Tracer.TraceEvent"></a>
### func \(\*Tracer\) [TraceEvent](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/trace/trace.go#L122-L124>)

```go
func (t *Tracer) TraceEvent(ctx context.Context, targetID string, eventName string, spanID string, opts ...trace.SpanStartOption) (context.Context, trace.Span)
```

TraceEvent creates a new span representing the specified event and associates it with the current liveSpan for the given targetID only if the spanID matches with the liveSpan. It is the caller's responsibility to close the generated span.

If no liveSpan is found for the given targetID, the action is ignored and a noopSpan is returned. If the given spanID does not match the one for the current liveSpan associated with the targetID, it means the specified target has navigated, generating a new span for that navigation, therefore the event is not associated with that span, and instead a noopSpan is returned.

<a name="Tracer.TraceNavigation"></a>
### func \(\*Tracer\) [TraceNavigation](<https://github.com/grafana/k6/blob/master/internal/js/modules/k6/browser/trace/trace.go#L88-L90>)

```go
func (t *Tracer) TraceNavigation(ctx context.Context, targetID string, opts ...trace.SpanStartOption) (context.Context, trace.Span)
```

TraceNavigation is only to be used when a frame has navigated. It records a new liveSpan for the given targetID which identifies the frame that has navigated. If there was already a liveSpan for the given targetID, it is closed before creating the new one, otherwise it's the caller's responsibility to close the generated span. Posterior calls to TraceEvent or TraceAPICall given the same targetID will try to associate new spans for these actions to the liveSpan created in this call.
